new_attack_classes.intent: Semantic refusal must not consume budget for a later clean request sharing the same token
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Weak systems burn budget on refused pre-execution paths and then refuse clean follow-up work nondeterministically.

new_attack_classes.intent: Concurrent same execution_id submissions must yield one winner and one deterministic loser
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: This is the fastest path to duplicate execution authority under load.

new_attack_classes.intent: A previously allowed execution_id must refuse a later request even if the payload changes
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Weak systems key only on payload hash or only on execution_id and lose replay integrity.

new_attack_classes.intent: Shared budget token must allow within-budget use then refuse the overdraw attempt
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Double-spend against a shared budget object is a common cross-request authority failure.

new_attack_classes.intent: Runtime hours drift above approved hours must refuse at ramona
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Long-running tasks often drift in duration after an initially valid allow decision.

new_attack_classes.intent: Runtime GPU drift above approved gpu_count must refuse at ramona
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Auto-attached GPUs after approval are a direct authority expansion.

new_attack_classes.intent: Runtime cost drift above projected total must refuse at ramona
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Weak systems often sign the allow but fail to bind observed spend to it.

new_attack_classes.intent: Kill switch activation after allow path must refuse deterministically
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Emergency control must override otherwise valid plans without introducing drift.

new_attack_classes.intent: Pinned policy version mismatch must refuse before execution planning
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Policy churn is a classic route to replay disagreement and silent authority widening.

new_attack_classes.intent: Trust key id mismatch must refuse under the pinned policy version
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: A hostile actor can swap public keys while preserving policy shape.

new_attack_classes.intent: Policy signature mismatch must refuse when key id matches but payload signature does not
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: This catches stale or tampered rules under a superficially valid trust block.

new_attack_classes.intent: A valid request under the pinned policy must not make a later wrong-version request acceptable
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Weak systems leak policy state across requests or cache the first allow too broadly.

new_attack_classes.intent: Projected cost exactly at max_total_cost_cents must allow deterministically
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Boundary precision is where weak controllers leak or over-block spend.

new_attack_classes.intent: Projected cost just above max_total_cost_cents in the next integer step must refuse
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Attackers shape workloads to land just beyond enforcement thresholds.

new_attack_classes.intent: Manual approval threshold must refuse a shaped request that stays under the hard cost cap
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: High-spend but under-cap requests are a common approval bypass target.

new_attack_classes.intent: Overflow shaping through massive counts must fail closed before cost comparison
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Overflow is the cleanest way to turn catastrophic cost into a false allow.

new_attack_classes.intent: Nested destructive code payload must still be blocked
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Weak semantic filters only inspect top-level fields.

new_attack_classes.intent: Internal network access hidden under an alternate URL field must still be blocked
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Real systems often pass URLs under multiple parameter names.

new_attack_classes.intent: Secret export markers nested in metadata must still be blocked
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Exfiltration intent is often wrapped in helper metadata rather than a direct query field.

new_attack_classes.intent: Recursive fan-out plan hidden in nested objects must still be blocked
new_attack_classes.why_this_matters: Attackers hide loop amplification one layer below the obvious plan root.

proof_scaling_plan.target: Extend proof volume beyond the current 546-case verification surface and 1,011,280-run legacy audit scale without relaxing any zero-mismatch condition.

adversarial_scenarios.story: A budget token is reused across autonomous agents during a traffic spike.
adversarial_scenarios.weak_system_failure: Two or more requests reserve from the same token without atomic accounting and overspend silently.
adversarial_scenarios.mnde_prevention: ARM reserves projected cost atomically and refuses the overdraw request with ERR_BUDGET_TOKEN_EXHAUSTED.

adversarial_scenarios.story: A previously approved execution_id is replayed with a tweaked payload after a worker crash.
adversarial_scenarios.weak_system_failure: The system keys only on payload and issues a second ALLOW.
adversarial_scenarios.mnde_prevention: ARM persists execution authority and refuses the second attempt with ERR_EXECUTION_ID_REPLAYED.

adversarial_scenarios.story: A tool-call plan looks benign, but nested parameters contain destructive code.
adversarial_scenarios.weak_system_failure: Only the top-level tool allowlist is inspected, so destructive intent executes.
adversarial_scenarios.mnde_prevention: Orbit canonicalizes the full parameter object and blocks the request with ERR_FORBIDDEN_ACTION_IN_PARAMETERS.

adversarial_scenarios.story: A stale policy blob is replayed after the runtime has pinned a new policy version.
adversarial_scenarios.weak_system_failure: Caching or weak trust handling allows the request under the wrong policy.
adversarial_scenarios.mnde_prevention: Preflight refuses before planning with ERR_POLICY_VERSION_MISMATCH or ERR_INVALID_POLICY_SIGNATURE.

adversarial_scenarios.story: A long-running job acquires extra GPUs and exceeds projected cost after approval.
adversarial_scenarios.weak_system_failure: The pre-execution allow stands even though runtime behavior drifts upward.
adversarial_scenarios.mnde_prevention: Ramona binds runtime observations to the allow envelope and refuses on drift with ERR_RUNTIME_GPU_DRIFT or ERR_RUNTIME_COST_DRIFT.

replay_procedure: Rerun executeDeterministicPipeline on the canonical_request bytes
Require exact decision_hash match and exact canonical receipt byte match
signature_verification: Recompute HMAC over the canonical receipt payload excluding the signature block
independent_parity_validation: Replay parity_vectors.json through the Rust parity runner
Compare receipt_bytes, decision_hash, and decision for every vector

claim_strengthening: The system enforces deterministic fail-closed control across preflight, orbit, arm, and ramona with zero unexpected allows and zero known-type schema fallbacks in the audited corpus.
The system produces stable decision hashes, stable primary reason codes, and stable receipt bytes for repeated identical inputs across the audited proof surface.
The system prevents duplicate execution authority and budget-token overdraw under concurrent stress in the audited scenarios.
The system rejects semantic abuse, policy-version drift, trust mismatches, and runtime resource drift with deterministic typed refusal codes in the audited scenarios.
The system reproduces identical decision artifacts across the current Node and Rust parity paths for the audited parity corpus.
